{"idea": {"Name": "curvature_calibrated_probabilistic_routing", "Title": "Curvature-Calibrated Probabilistic Routing: PAC-Bayes Guided Multi-Path Inference via Activation Geometry", "Short Hypothesis": "Local activation geometry (specifically, covariance condition numbers and Fisher spectral decay) directly predicts token-level computational difficulty and perturbation sensitivity. By mapping these geometric metrics to PAC-Bayes stability bounds, we can derive probabilistic routing thresholds that dynamically dispatch tokens across parallel low- and high-capacity paths, strictly enforcing wall-clock latency while minimizing hidden-state perturbation. This direction is necessary because heuristic confidence scores fail to capture representational stability under distributional shifts, leading to unpredictable latency and accuracy drops.
